notes: Baseline created 6 stories (vs 3 with skill) — split search by individual field rather than combining
Most assertions pass for both configurations — they test structural compliance, not quality. The skill's value is more qualitative (story decomposition quality, AC specificity).
With-skill produces fewer, more focused stories (3-4 per eval vs 6-9 baseline) — better vertical slicing.
Only discriminating assertion: vertical slicing check caught a layer-split story in JWT baseline.
With-skill uses ~20% more tokens on average, likely due to reading the SKILL.md and template.
Qualitative review needed to assess AC specificity and whether stories are truly implementation-ready.
